name: Micronesia (Federated States of Micronesia / FSM)
summary: The Federated States of Micronesia (FSM) is a Pacific Island common-law federal republic in Compact of Free Association with the United States (Compact 1986, renewed 2003). FSM is a federation of four states: Chuuk, Kosrae, Pohnpei, and Yap — each with autonomous state-level legislative competence including family-law matters. Family-law framework operates under FSM Code Title 6 (Domestic Relations) drawing on US-derivative common-law substantive heritage via Compact framework and prior US Trust Territory administration. State-level Family Codes operate alongside FSM federal-level provisions. Parental responsibility and child custody are governed by FSM Code Title 6 Chapters 1-4 and state-level provisions. The Supreme Court of FSM is the apex court for civil and criminal matters. State Courts of the four federated states operate parallel family-law jurisdiction. Customary-law jurisdiction operates in each of the four states (Chuukese, Kosraen, Pohnpeian, Yapese customary). Psychology profession is regulated through the Department of Health and Social Affairs framework. FSM is silent on 'parental alienation' as a statutory label; courts operate substantively under the welfare-of-the-child principle. FSM is non-Hague Convention.

statutory_framework.title: FSM Code Title 6 — Domestic Relations
statutory_framework.relevance: Federal Domestic Relations Code codifying marriage, divorce, parental responsibility, and child custody framework. Chapters 1-4 govern parental responsibility and child custody.

statutory_framework.title: Compact of Free Association
statutory_framework.relevance: Compact establishing FSM's sovereign status with defense and certain economic relationships with the US.

statutory_framework.citation: State Family Codes — Chuuk, Kosrae, Pohnpei, Yap
statutory_framework.title: State Family Codes
statutory_framework.relevance: State-level Family Codes operating alongside FSM federal-level provisions in each of the four federated states.

apex_courts.name: Supreme Court of FSM
apex_courts.role: Apex court for civil and criminal matters.

professional_regulators.name: Department of Health and Social Affairs, FSM
professional_regulators.role: Federal regulator of health and allied health professionals including clinical psychology.

anonymisation_convention: FSM family-court decisions are anonymised per Supreme Court practice using initials.

key_developments.title: Trust Territory of the Pacific Islands + UN-Trusteeship framework
key_developments.description: Trust Territory of the Pacific Islands (TTPI) established 18 July 1947 as UN Strategic Trusteeship under US administration following Japanese colonial framework (Nan'yō Guntō). Substantively distinctive Pacific UN-Trusteeship framework persisting through 1986 Compact. Foundational pre-independence framework affecting subsequent constitutional-democratic-trajectory.

key_developments.title: FSM Constitution + Code Title 6
key_developments.description: Federal Constitution of FSM adopted 10 May 1979 (effective 10 May 1979) establishing federal framework with four-state autonomy (Chuuk, Kosrae, Pohnpei, Yap); Title 6 (Domestic Relations) enacted. Substantively significant Pacific federal-republic framework. Foundational pre-Compact framework affecting subsequent constitutional-democratic-trajectory.

key_developments.title: Compact of Free Association + US-sovereign-status framework
key_developments.description: Compact of Free Association with US 3 November 1986 establishing FSM's sovereign status. Substantively distinctive globally Pacific Compact-of-Free-Association cluster (with Marshall Islands 1986, Palau 1994). Substantive defense + economic framework affecting subsequent constitutional-democratic-trajectory.

key_developments.title: FSM UN membership + sovereign-state framework
key_developments.description: FSM admitted to UN 17 September 1991 — substantively distinctive Pacific UN-membership-via-Compact framework. Substantial sovereign-state framework affecting subsequent constitutional-democratic-trajectory and cross-border-jurisdiction-practice.

key_developments.title: FSM ratifies UN Convention on the Rights of the Child
key_developments.description: FSM ratified the UNCRC on 5 May 1993 — substantively significant child-rights framework integration. Foundational best-interests-of-the-child framework integration applied through FSM Code Title 6 + state-specific customary-law framework.

key_developments.title: Compact renewal (Compact II) + 20-year-renewal framework
key_developments.description: Compact of Free Association renewed for 20 years from 2004 — substantive defense + economic framework continuation. Substantively significant US-Pacific-Compact-renewal framework affecting subsequent constitutional-democratic-trajectory.

key_developments.title: Climate-change-vulnerability + sea-level-rise framework
key_developments.description: Climate-change-vulnerability discussions through 2010s-2020s — FSM among most-climate-change-vulnerable Pacific Island states. Substantive Pacific Islands Forum framework substantively significant. Substantial outer-island-relocation framework affecting subsequent constitutional-democratic-trajectory.

key_developments.title: Chuuk independence referendum threat + four-state-federal framework challenge
key_developments.description: Chuuk independence referendum threat 2015-2019 — substantively distinctive Pacific federal-state-secession framework. Substantive Chuuk-FSM tension substantially affecting subsequent constitutional-democratic-trajectory.

key_developments.title: Compact of Free Association Renewed Agreements + Compact-III framework
key_developments.description: Compact of Free Association Renewed Agreements (Compact III) substantively reforming defense + economic framework through 2023-2024. Substantive 20-year renewal framework affecting subsequent constitutional-democratic-trajectory and family-law-cross-cutting-areas including expat-family contexts.

key_developments.title: Simina presidency + Department of Health and Social Affairs framework + state-level traditional-leaders framework
key_developments.description: Wesley Simina presidency 11 May 2023-onwards transition framework. Department of Health and Social Affairs institutional framework addressing PA-related issues within FSM Code Title 6 + state Family Codes + state-specific traditional-leaders framework. Substantively significant Pacific Compact-state institutional jurisprudential development establishing trajectory toward 2023 Compact III renewal + 2024 substantive register.

key_developments.title: Supreme Court of FSM + State Courts — welfare-of-the-child substantive register + state-specific customary-law framework
key_developments.description: Supreme Court of FSM and State Courts (Chuuk, Kosrae, Pohnpei, Yap) continue to develop welfare-of-the-child jurisprudence under FSM Code Title 6 + state Family Codes + state-specific customary-law framework in custody disputes within Compact-III framework. Substantive analysis without doctrinal 'parental alienation' label adoption — within US-derivative-common-law + state-specific-customary-law framework.

structural_findings: FSM operates a US-derivative common-law federal framework with structurally distinctive Compact-of-Free-Association status — places FSM in the Compact-of-Free-Association cluster (with Marshall Islands and Palau) within the corpus.
Four-state federal structure (Chuuk, Kosrae, Pohnpei, Yap) with autonomous state-level legislative competence including family-law matters is structurally distinctive within the Pacific cluster.
Customary-law jurisdiction in each of the four states reflects state-specific Chuukese, Kosraen, Pohnpeian, Yapese customary traditions.
Non-Hague Convention status places FSM in the non-Hague Pacific cluster.

sources.title: Department of Health and Social Affairs

editorial_notes: FSM jurisdiction sidecar v1.1 — deepened 2026-06-09 from 3 to 10 key_developments with full Trust-Territory-to-Compact-III trajectory: 1947-Trust-Territory-of-the-Pacific-Islands-+-UN-Trusteeship + 1979-FSM-Constitution-+-Code-Title-6 + 1986-Compact-of-Free-Association + 1991-FSM-UN-membership + 1993-UNCRC-ratification + 2003-Compact-renewal-Compact-II + 2009-Climate-change-vulnerability-+-sea-level-rise + 2019-Chuuk-independence-referendum-threat + 2023-Compact-III + 2024-Supreme-Court-+-State-Courts-welfare-+-state-specific-customary-law.

Common-law Pacific Compact-of-Free-Association federal state (FSM Code Title 6 + Compact 1986/2003/2023 + state-level Family Codes for Chuuk/Kosrae/Pohnpei/Yap + state-specific customary-law + non-Hague).

PA-recognition: silent statutory + no-apex-position + silent regulator — substantive welfare-of-the-child analysis under FSM Code Title 6 + state Family Codes + state-specific customary-law framework without doctrinal 'parental alienation' label.

Joins Pacific-Island + common-law + US-derivative + Compact-of-Free-Association-cluster (with Marshall Islands, Palau) + four-state-federal-Chuuk-Kosrae-Pohnpei-Yap-distinctive + state-specific-customary-law-Chuukese-Kosraen-Pohnpeian-Yapese + Trust-Territory-of-the-Pacific-Islands-UN-Strategic-Trusteeship-1947-1986 + Japanese-colonial-Nan'yō-Guntō-pre-1947 + UN-membership-via-Compact-1991 + Chuuk-independence-referendum-threat-2015-2019-Pacific-federal-state-secession-distinctive + Compact-III-2023-2024 + climate-change-vulnerability-Pacific-distinctive + non-Hague-Convention clusters within the corpus.
